howItWorks.intro: This freelance hourly rate calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Hourly Rate
rules.explanation: Target income grossed up for expenses divided by billable hours.

howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: How do I set my freelance rate?
faq.answer: Start with your desired salary, add expenses and taxes, divide by billable hours.

faq.question: What percentage of hours are billable?
faq.answer: Typically only 50-65% of working hours are billable - the rest is admin, marketing, etc.

faq.question: Is the Freelance Hourly Rate Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard financial planning formulas trusted by individuals and families. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What target annual income should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate target annual income value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Freelance Hourly Rate Calculator?
sections.body: The Freelance Hourly Rate Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your target annual income, billable hours per week, weeks off per year, you get instant results including minimum hourly rate, day rate, weekly rate. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting minimum hourly rate right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your target annual income and need to find the right minimum hourly rate. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Target Annual Income ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your target annual income — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: billable hours per week, weeks off per year, business expenses. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your minimum hourly rate, day rate, weekly rate. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your target annual income before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both minimum hourly rate and day rate — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to manage your finances.
If you're unsure about your business expenses, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
